notes:
A polish release for the gutter affordances on bulleted lists (Addendum 19 §S2).

**Fixes & polish**

- **The hover marker now works on nested bullets at every level.** On a bullet
  inside another bullet, the marker, collapse chevron, click-to-zoom, drag, and
  right-click menu were unreliable or dead — moving toward the bullet would flip
  the marker to the bullet's *parent*. Hovering anywhere on a row — the gutter,
  the bullet, the gap before the text, the text itself — now reliably targets
  that row's own block, at any nesting depth.
- **A click on the marker always zooms** (it no longer gets swallowed as a
  no-op drag when the pointer drifts a couple of pixels — common on a trackpad
  or when aiming at the small marker).
- **Right-clicking a bullet opens its menu.** Right-clicking the bullet glyph
  used to do nothing on a top-level bullet and target the wrong item on a nested
  one; it now opens the block menu for the bullet you clicked.

**Under the hood**

- The gutter overlay and the block context menu resolve the acted-upon block by
  document coordinates (ProseMirror `posAtCoords`) instead of the DOM element
  under the pointer. A list item's inline paragraph and the nested list it
  contains both carry their own ids, so element-based resolution mis-targeted
  the bullet's parent; coordinate resolution maps the whole row to the correct
  item regardless of nesting.
